domains.label: left
domains.notes: Consistent progressive framing on social, economic, and political stories; strong advocacy tone across multiple issue areas

domains.label: lean-left
domains.notes: UK-based but significant US coverage; editorial line favors progressive climate, social, and economic positions; news reporting is generally fair but opinion section pulls left

domains.label: lean-left
domains.notes: News reporting aims for balance but source selection, story framing, and editorial page lean consistently center-left; opinion columnists skew left

domains.label: lean-left
domains.notes: News coverage generally professional; editorial and opinion sections lean left-of-center; source emphasis and framing choices reflect a center-left institutional perspective

domains.label: center
domains.notes: Public broadcaster with editorial standards requiring balance; coverage spans diverse perspectives; occasionally critiqued from both sides — a reliable center signal

domains.label: center
domains.notes: Wire service with strict factual-reporting standards; minimal editorial voice; consistently cited as a low-bias reference point across media-literacy research

domains.label: center
domains.notes: Non-profit wire service; fact-checking arm widely respected; coverage is consistently straight-news with very low partisan framing

domains.label: lean-right
domains.notes: News desk maintains professional standards; opinion and editorial pages are clearly right-of-center with pro-market, conservative-leaning commentary

domains.label: right
domains.notes: Opinion programming dominates primetime; news-side reporting often incorporates conservative framing; editorial line consistently supports Republican policy positions

domains.label: right
domains.notes: Explicitly nationalist-populist editorial line; story selection and framing serve right-wing political agenda; limited news-side / opinion separation

domains.label: mixed
domains.notes: DC-focused outlet with both left-leaning and right-leaning opinion columnists; news coverage is relatively balanced; signal depends heavily on section (opinion vs news)
